summary: REQ-1 is implemented via a new token self-check function in `frontend/src/config/tokens.ts` comparing configured vs expected mainnet ledger IDs. REQ-2 and REQ-3 appear largely implemented via a new Dashboard diagnostics panel, a React Query hook, and a read-only ICRC metadata client; however, due to truncation of the diagnostics panel file, some UI acceptance criteria (displaying returned metadata values like decimals and per-token error states) cannot be fully verified from the provided diff summary.

missing_requirements.requirement: For each configured token, the diagnostics UI shows whether the metadata check succeeded and displays returned values (at minimum decimals) when available; and shows a non-crashing per-token error state while still allowing copying diagnostics JSON including the error.
missing_requirements.reason: Backend/read-only metadata fetching and JSON copy structure are implemented, but the UI rendering of per-token metadata success/failure, returned values (e.g., decimals), and per-token error state is not visible in the truncated `TokenLedgerDiagnosticsPanel.tsx` snippet, so this cannot be confirmed as implemented.
